methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Impulse Labs is a Series A climate and energy company that has raised $25M to date. The valuation analysis benchmarks against a peer set of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), with particular focus on three same-stage, same-sector peers: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach, using total raised as a proxy base and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yields $50M. The peer-median method anchors to the $150M median valuation across the three directly comparable companies, with observed range of $110M to $200M. This reflects the market pricing for similarly-staged frontier energy technology ventures.

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $42M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $100M. The wide dispersion—driven by material differences between bottom-up stage progression and peer-market benchmarking—reflects genuine uncertainty in pricing early-stage climate tech. Key variables include relative product maturity versus peer cohort, capital efficiency metrics, and technology differentiation versus Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy. The peer-median anchor at $150M likely represents market consensus, though the stage-progression floor at $42M suggests downside if the company lags peer performance benchmarks.
